articles.title: Bihar Heatwave: Patna Schools Up to Class 8 Closed Till June 30; DM Issues Fresh Order - Times Now
articles.full_text:
The Patna district administration has suspended academic activities for all government and private schools, including pre-schools, up to Class 8 till June 30 in view of the continuing heatwave and soaring temperatures. The order, issued by District Magistrate Kundan Kumar, aims to protect children from the adverse effects of extreme weather conditions.
According to the official order issued on June 28, the district administration observed that the prevailing high temperatures continue to pose a serious risk to the health and safety of school-going children. Citing the persistence of heatwave conditions across the district, the administration decided to extend restrictions on physical academic activities.
The directive has been issued under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023. It follows an earlier order issued on June 21, 2026, under Memo No. 8706/Legal, which had already imposed restrictions in view of the intense heat.
Schools Directed to Reschedule Academic Activities
Under the latest order, academic activities for students from pre-school to Class 8 in all government and private schools across Patna district will remain prohibited till June 30, 2026. The order comes into effect from June 29 and will remain applicable through June 30.
The district administration has directed all school authorities to reschedule their academic activities in conformity with the fresh order. While regular classes for the affected grades have been suspended during this period, the order primarily focuses on ensuring that schools modify their functioning in line with the administration's directions.
Decision Taken to Protect Children's Health
In the order, District Magistrate Kundan Kumar stated that the decision was taken after it appeared that the prevailing high temperatures continued to endanger the health and lives of children. With heatwave conditions still persisting across the district, the administration considered it necessary to prohibit academic activities for younger students as a precautionary measure.
The administration has advised all concerned school authorities to strictly comply with the directions during the specified period.
The move comes amid continuing high temperatures in several parts of Bihar, with authorities taking precautionary measures to minimise heat-related risks for schoolchildren.

articles.title: Bihar School Holidays: Patna Schools Shut Till June 30 for Classes 1 to 8 Amid Heatwave - Jagran Josh
articles.full_text:
Bihar School Holidays: Patna Schools Shut Till June 30 for Classes 1 to 8 Amid Heatwave
Bihar School Holidays: Patna Schools will be shut till June 30 for Classes 1 to 8 Amid Heatwave. The decision is being announced by the DM Kundan Kumar by seeing the extreme heatwave conditions and keeping childrens safety in mind.
Bihar School Holidays: Patna Schools will be shut till June 30 for Classes 1 to 8 due to extreme Heatwave. The decision is being announced by the DM Kundan Kumar by seeing the extreme heat conditions across state and keeping childrens safety in mind. Schools to be rescheduled the academic work from June 29 till June 30. While regular classses for the other classes has been suspended during this period until further notice.
In the order, DM Kundan Kumar has stated this decision after seeing the high temperature of Bihar. Students safety and their health is priority so during this periods all the academic acativities can pe postponed as a precautionary measure. This derictive has been issued under section 163 Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 202023.
Heatwave Precautions To be taken by School, Parents and Students:
Due to the severe heat condition in Bihar, it is important for everyone to take precautionary measures to stay safe in this boiling temperature.
- Schools to be have well functioned classroom with fan/ac and more
- Water cooler to be placed for easy access and for staying hydrated
- Plan of more indoor activities rather than planning it out door
- Medical facility to be provided or arranged for any situation
- Assemblies can be shifted from open grounds to auditoriums
- Children to have a balanced diet and more of water based supplements
- Wear light shades instead of dark ones
- Basic safety measures for traveling home to schools and vice versa.
It has been instructed all the government and private institutions to strictly follow the orders and to adopt the safety measures. It is always advised to stay updated with the official notifications and announcements. Parents and students can check their school official groups to latest notice and school holidays.
Anisha Mishra is journalist with over 3 years of experience in covering the Indian education sector. She has worked extensively in the K12 domain, with focus on the state board as well as central board examinations, policy structure of seconday and higher secondary education as well as the entrance examinations like JEE, NEET, CLAT, etc. Her extensive experience in the domain has helped her provide students with concise accurate information in all aspectes of school life and education. Her key interest lies in decoding the changes in the curriculum, NEP implementation and changing education ecosystem in the country. Besides working, she enjoys traveling, exploring new places and cultures, and painting.
